source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, South Pass Block 60

actions_taken: Crew immediately stopped work and reported to Fieldwood Energy personnel
Injured person was pulled out by crew
Fieldwood Energy issued Safe & Sound stand down on Open Holes
event.summary: On 5 June 2019 at 0915 hours, a construction employee partially fell through a corroded section of deck grating on the Fieldwood Energy Offshore LLC South Pass 60-D platform while retrieving a wrench for scaffold installation. The employee caught himself with his arms and elbows, sustaining minor scratches. The hole measured 16 inches long by 20 inches wide. Investigation revealed severe grating corrosion caused by inadequate preventive maintenance, lack of operator training on corrosion hazard identification, and possible accelerated corrosion from a nearby gas flowline producing condensation.
recommendations: Management and facility supervisors should ensure operators are provided training to identify imminent safety hazards, critical areas of concern, and corrosion protection system grades

hazards.name: Structural corrosion of deck grating and platform components
Condensation from gas flowline accelerating corrosion

threats.name: Failure to identify and document corrosion deficiencies during inspections
Failure to barricade or repair corroded areas identified in surveys
Inadequate operator training on corrosion hazard identification
Inconsistent use of safety checklists and documentation procedures
Lack of communication of survey findings to field personnel

consequences.name: Potential fatality from fall to water (30-40 feet below)

controls.name: API RP 2A Level 1 structural survey
controls.lod_basis: Inspection and assessment of structural integrity

supporting_text: The BSEE team reviewed Fieldwood Energy's 2018 API RP 2A Level 1 survey. The survey stated, 'heavy active scaling and blistering to 70% of the underside of main deck and 90% of affected areas suffering metal loss.'
BSEE found that Fieldwood failed to address the areas of concern from this survey or barricade the areas of decking with metal loss.

controls.name: Ultrasonic Testing (UT) inspections
controls.barrier_role: Detection of metal loss and corrosion severity
controls.lod_basis: Non-destructive testing to assess material thickness

supporting_text: During the interviews, Fieldwood Energy Operators stated, although contractors performed Level 1 surveys and Ultrasonic Testing (UT) inspections, the Area Foreman does not receive copies of the surveys.

controls.name: Weekly platform safety checklist
controls.barrier_role: Identification and documentation of platform deficiencies
controls.lod_basis: Routine inspection and documentation of safety conditions

supporting_text: The team reviewed the Fieldwood safety checklist dated May 17, May 24, and May 31, 2019. The team noted that the corroded grating and handrails on the checklist were marked in 'good condition'.
BSEE found that Fieldwood did not consistently use the latest revision of the checklist.
Fieldwood used versions 0, 2, and 4 indicating there is inconsistency in the use of the most recent version.

controls.name: Operator training on corrosion hazard identification
controls.barrier_role: Enable personnel to recognize imminent safety hazards from corrosion

supporting_text: BSEE concluded that Fieldwood did not provide the operators on the platform with proper training on the identification of imminent safety hazards regarding corrosion assessment of production vessels, piping, handrails, decking, and fasteners.
The Area Foreman stated he was not aware of anyone under his supervision offshore, aside from third party surveyors, who have corrosion assessment training.
Despite providing no corrosion assessment training, Fieldwood's JSA requires operators to identify and mitigate every hazard, including corroded decking.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) review and sign-off
controls.barrier_role: Identify and mitigate hazards before work begins
controls.lod_basis: Pre-job hazard assessment and crew awareness

supporting_text: On the morning of the incident, the Fluid Crane construction crew conducted a safety meeting with Fieldwood Operators. During the safety meeting, the crew discussed erecting three independent scaffolding systems on SP 60-D platform. The crew assessed the jobsite deeming it sufficient to set up scaffolding.
After assessing the jobsite, the crew reviewed and signed a Job Safety Analysis (JSA).
The crew had crossed directly over the corroded grating several times that morning without noticing a potential open hole.

controls.name: Preventive maintenance and repair of corroded structures
controls.barrier_role: Eliminate or reduce corrosion hazards through timely repairs
controls.lod_basis: Maintenance program to address identified deficiencies

supporting_text: Equipment Failure - Inadequate preventive maintenance: Fieldwood Energy failed to correct the corrosion areas of concern documented in the 2018 API RP 2A Level 1 survey inspection report.
BSEE found that Fieldwood failed to address the areas of concern from this survey or barricade the areas of decking with metal loss.

controls.name: Barricading of corroded deck areas
controls.barrier_role: Prevent access to hazardous corroded areas
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to prevent personnel exposure to open holes

supporting_text: BSEE found that Fieldwood failed to address the areas of concern from this survey or barricade the areas of decking with metal loss.

controls.name: Communication of survey findings to field personnel
controls.barrier_role: Ensure Area Foreman and operators are aware of identified deficiencies

supporting_text: During the interviews, Fieldwood Energy Operators stated, although contractors performed Level 1 surveys and Ultrasonic Testing (UT) inspections, the Area Foreman does not receive copies of the surveys. The Area Foreman stated he only receives information on platform repairs and timing of construction crews.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
